figures.caption: Fig. 1: Overview of the proposed learning-based navigation framework. The pipeline includes data collection, global path planning, local motion planning, deployment, model training, and evaluation.
Fig. 2: Overview of the proposed navigation framework.
Fig. 4: MLP-based behavior-cloning architecture for DWA candidate selection from context and candidate vectors.
Fig. 5: Overview of PPO fine-tuning for feasibility-aware DWA candidate selection.

Fig. 8: Qualitative comparison in the static scenario. The Learning-Based DWA trajectory and profiles are shown in blue, whereas those of the conventional DWA are shown in orange. The Learning-Based DWA achieves better path adherence and lower tracking error, while the conventional DWA reaches the goal faster.

Fig. 9: Qualitative comparison in the obstacle scenario: Learning-Based DWA (blue) and conventional DWA (orange). The Learning-Based DWA initiates obstacle avoidance earlier, leading to a slightly larger tracking error but a shorter path and smoother angular motion.
